title: Divad
content:
{{Top|leg|italics=1}}
'''''Divad''''' was an [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] used by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

''Divad'' and the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvettes]] ''[[BB 45]]'' and ''[[SB 35]]'' formed the escort force of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]] and were specifically tasked with protecting the [[communications satellite]] coordinating its defenses in the [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]].

In preparation for their [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|assault on the battle station]], [[Keyan Farlander/Legends|Keyan Farlander]], his two [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] wingmen and an escort of two [[R-22 Spearhead starfighter|R-22 Spearheads]] were dispatched to destroy the comsat. While Farlander's wingmen held off Imperial fighters, Farlander ignored the threat and headed straight for the satellite, destroying it with a proton torpedo before quickly returning to base. However, both his wingmen were shot down and the pilots had to be rescued later.

The destruction of the comsat denied access to Imperial reinforcements and left it open to direct attack by unsupported starfighters. It also succeeded in slowing down the Empire's preparations for the battle, allowing the Alliance longer to analyze the [[Death Star plans/Legends|Death Star plans]] brought to them by [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] and to plan their strategy.

==Behind the scenes==
The Death Star's support fleet is not shown in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] and no other canon sources say that such forces ever encountered Rebel fighters before the attack on the Death Star. It is possible that the escort had already left when we see the scene of the fleet moving to the Death Star or that the over-confident Empire held the support ships back so as not to distract from the Death Star's expected victory. Either way, the main bulk of the Rebel fleet would be free to move without further encounters.

Note that in ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]'', we see that the Death Star was orbited by an unnamed [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] that launched waves of TIEs to the [[Blue Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Blue Squadron]] ships. It is possible that this was also part of the support fleet although, because of ''Rebel Assault''{{'s}} ambiguous canonicity, this event may not be canonical.

Divad is perhaps an anagram for David.

==Appearances==
*[[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']]
